steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: Inside the parentheses, $x^2-100y^2$ is a difference of squares: $(x-10y)(x+10y)$. Therefore:$$11(x^2-100y^2)=11(x-10y)(x+10y)$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The factored form is $11(x-10y)(x+10y)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
